chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي بِالْقَوْمِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى غَيْرِ طُهْرٍ أَوْ لِغَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 54 – A man prays Salāt with the people (leading them) and he is upon without cleanliness, or towards other than the Qiblah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/267)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/267)
text: قَوْمٍ خَرَجُوا مِنْ خُرَاسَانَ أَوْ بَعْضِ الْجِبَالِ وَ كَانَ يَؤُمُّهُمْ رَجُلٌ فَلَمَّا صَارُوا إِلَى الْكُوفَةِ عَلِمُوا أَنَّهُ يَهُودِيٌّ قَالَ لَا يُعِيدُونَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a group who go out from Khurasan or one of the mountains, and a man had led them (in Salāt). So when they came to be in Al-Kufa he let them know that he was a Jew’. Heasws said: ‘They would not be repeating (the Salāt)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “About the case of a people who traveled from certain mountainous area of Khurasan and a man would lead them in Salah (prayer), when in al-Kufah they found out that he was a Jew, Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘They are not required to perform their Salah (prayer) again.’”
